note:
美国联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)正在就Charge的请愿书进行新一轮的公开评论，该请愿书要求允许对宽带用户设置数据上限，并向在线视频提供商收取网络互连费用。此前，一项法院裁决可能会使FCC的决定复杂化。
对Charge的请愿书发表意见的最后期限已于8月6日过去。但在今天发布的一份公告中，联邦通信委员会表示，它将开启一个额外的评议期，将持续到9月2日，让人们有时间就法院裁决的影响发表意见。
FCC的通知称，为了确保[有线竞争]局有完整的记录来评估这些条件的影响，我们启动了这一额外的评议期，同时也邀请评论者就FCC对宪章请愿书的审议发表新的法院裁决的影响。和以前一样，您可以通过点击“新归档”或“快速归档”来提交评论。目前已有1500多份申请，其中大部分来自反对数据上限的消费者。
周五，美国哥伦比亚特区巡回上诉法院的裁决取消了一项合并条件，该条件要求查特在2023年5月18日之前向大型在线提供商提供免费网络互联。奥巴马时代的联邦通信委员会在2016年收购时代华纳有线电视和光明之家网络时，对查特施加了这一条件和其他条件，目的是防止在公司拒绝支付ISP要求的费用时，有损害消费者宽带性能的商业纠纷。但特朗普时代的FCC在主席阿吉特·派(Ajit Pai)的领导下，选择不在法庭上为合并条件的优点辩护，为新的裁决铺平了道路。
这项裁决是在查特向FCC请愿让禁止数据上限和网络互联支付的合并条件提前两年即2021年5月到期两个月后做出的。这是根据合并批准的原始条款可以取消这些条件的最早日期。周五的法院裁决似乎使查特的请愿书在网络互联条件下变得毫无意义，但联邦通信委员会仍然至少要决定是否取消数据上限禁令。
派在担任委员时投票反对合并条件，但还没有担任董事长，现在他有机会提前两年让查特脱身。即使有额外的评议期，FCC在拟议的2021年5月到期日之前也有充足的时间。派可能会试图在今年年底之前推动对查特请愿书的裁决，因为乔·拜登(Joe Biden)在总统选举中的潜在胜利将使民主党最早在明年1月获得联邦通信委员会(FCC)的多数席位。
Charge是仅次于康卡斯特的美国第二大有线电视公司，它以Spectrum品牌在41个州提供服务。Charge表示，目前它不打算对互联设置数据上限或收费，但该公司最近在一份文件中试图说服FCC，设置数据上限的家庭互联网计划经常受到宽带用户的欢迎。
在周五的一份文件中，查特确认它已经遵守了网络互联的要求。但由于法院已经取消了这一合并条件，理论上查特甚至可以在FCC发布裁决之前就开始要求在线视频提供商付款。在周五法院裁决后，查特没有回应置评请求。
披露：拥有Charge 13%股份的Advance/Newhouse伙伴关系是Advance Publications的一部分。Advance Publications拥有康泰纳仕(CondéNast)，康泰纳仕拥有Ars Technica。
note_en:
The Federal Communications Commission is taking another round of public comments on Charter's petition seeking permission to impose data caps on broadband users and charge network-interconnection fees to online-video providers, following a court ruling that may complicate the FCC's decision.
 The deadline for comments on Charter's petition passed on August 6. But in a  public notice issued today, the FCC said it is opening an additional comment period that will last until September 2, giving people time to weigh in on the impact of the court ruling.
 "To ensure that the [Wireline Competition] Bureau has a full record upon which to evaluate the effects of the conditions, we initiate this additional comment period," the FCC notice said, while also inviting commenters to "address the effect" of the new court ruling on the FCC's consideration of Charter's petition. As before, comments can be submitted on the  docket by clicking "New Filing" or "Express." There are more than 1,500 filings, mostly from consumers who object to data caps.
  The  ruling Friday by the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit eliminated a merger condition that required Charter to provide free network interconnection to large online providers until May 18, 2023. The Obama-era FCC imposed that and other conditions on Charter when it bought Time Warner Cable and Bright House Networks in 2016, with the goal of preventing business disputes that have a history of  harming consumer-broadband performance when companies refuse to pay fees demanded by ISPs. But the Trump-era FCC under Chairman Ajit Pai chose not to defend the merits of the merger conditions in court, paving the way for the new ruling.
 The ruling came two months after Charter  petitioned the FCC to let the merger conditions banning data caps and network-interconnection payments expire two years early, in May 2021. That's the earliest date on which the conditions can be eliminated under the original terms of the merger approval. Friday's court ruling seems to render Charter's petition moot as far as the network-interconnection condition goes, but the FCC still has to at least decide whether to lift the data-cap prohibition.
 Pai voted against the merger conditions when he was a commissioner but not yet the chairman, and now he has a chance to let Charter off the hook two years early. Even with the extra comment period, the FCC has plenty of time before the proposed May 2021 expiration date. Pai would likely try to push through a ruling on Charter's petition by the end of this year because a potential Joe Biden victory in the presidential election would give Democrats the FCC majority as early as January.
 Charter is the second-biggest cable company in the United States after Comcast, and it offers service in 41 states under the Spectrum brand name. Charter has said it doesn't "currently" plan to impose data caps or charge for interconnection, but the company recently  tried to convince the FCC in a filing that home-Internet plans with data caps are "often popular" with broadband users.
 In a  filing on Friday, Charter confirmed that it has complied with the network-interconnection requirement. But with the court having vacated that merger condition, Charter could theoretically start asking online-video providers for payments even before the FCC issues its decision. Charter did not respond to a request for comment after Friday's court ruling.
 Disclosure: The Advance/Newhouse Partnership, which owns 13 percent of Charter, is part of Advance Publications. Advance Publications owns Condé Nast, which owns Ars Technica.
title: FCC要求就是否让查特设定数据上限提供更多公众意见
title_en: FCC asks for more public input on whether to let Charter impose data caps

note:
这家初创公司的前身是雷神货运公司(Thor Trucking)，它从一群投资者那里筹集了资金，其中包括Proeza Ventures和Build Capital Group。Proeza Ventures是一家专注于移动业务的风险投资公司，由Metalsa的控股公司支持。随着资本的增加，XOS还增加了一些新的董事会成员。Proeza Ventures的鲁道夫·埃利亚斯·迪克(Rodolfo Elias Dieck)和戴姆勒(Daimler)前高管、现供职于Build Capital的马克·兰伯特(Mark Lampert)都加入了董事会。XOS也加强了高管队伍，包括聘请金斯利·阿夫米克(Kingsley Afemikhe)担任首席财务官，聘请特斯拉(Tesla)头号员工兼科学总监罗布·费伯(Rob Ferber)担任首席技术官。
Xos卡车的联合创始人兼首席执行官达科塔·塞姆勒说：“我们很高兴能继续扩大我们的业务，为我们的客户提供同类中最好的最后一英里电动汽车。”“我们的目标是提供可靠、负担得起和可持续的交通工具，因为电子商务需求量在不断增加，并在大流行期间加速增长。”
资金将用于扩大业务和扩大其电动滑板底盘的生产，该底盘是为6级卡车设计的，这是一种中型商用车，通常用于在人口稠密的城市地区进行最后一英里的送货。这款滑板被称为X-Platform，旨在适应各种中等负荷的车身、轴距和长达200英里的航程要求。总部位于墨西哥的汽车供应商Metalsa帮助Xos进行了设计，并为底盘提供零部件。
据UPS称，过去八个月，UPS在洛杉矶地区的客户路线上一直使用XOS车辆。Loomis是Xos的现有客户，在2019年的试点计划之后又订购了20辆卡车。
note_en:
The startup, which was formerly known as Thor Trucking, raised the funds from a group of investors including Proeza Ventures, a mobility-focused VC firm backed by Metalsa’s holding company, and BUILD Capital Group. Xos also gained a few new board members along with the capital. Rodolfo Elias Dieck of Proeza Ventures and Mark Lampert, a former Daimler executive who is now at Build Capital, have joined the board. Xos has beefed up its executive ranks as well, including hiring Kingsley Afemikhe as its CFO and Rob Ferber, employee number one and science director at Tesla, as its CTO.
 “We’re excited to continue growing our operations to provide best-in-class last-mile electric vehicles for our customers,” said Dakota Semler, co-founder and CEO of Xos Trucks. “It’s our goal to provide reliable, affordable, and sustainable transportation as the volume of e-commerce demands are increasing, and have accelerated during the pandemic.”
  Funding will be used to expand operations and scale up production of its electric skateboard chassis that is designed for Class 6 trucks, the medium-duty commercial vehicles that are often used in last-mile delivery in dense urban areas. This skateboard, known as the X-Platform, was designed to accommodate a variety of medium-duty bodies, wheelbases and range requirements up to 200 miles. Metalsa, the Mexico-based automotive supplier, helped Xos with the design and is providing components to the chassis.
 Xos vehicles have been used by UPS on customer routes in the Los Angeles area for the past eight months, according to the company. Loomis, an existing customer of Xos, has order another 20 trucks following a pilot program in 2019.
title: XOS卡车筹集2000万美元将更多的电动商用卡车投入使用
title_en: Xos Trucks raises $20M to put more of its electric commercial trucks on the road

note:
日内瓦/马尼拉(路透社)-世界卫生组织(世卫组织)总干事Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus周二表示，囤积可能的新冠肺炎疫苗而将其他疫苗排除在外的国家将加深大流行，并发出最后的呼吁，呼吁各国加入全球疫苗协定。
世卫组织要求较富裕国家在8月31日之前加入“Covax全球疫苗基金”，与发展中国家分享有望获得疫苗的人。特德罗斯说，他给世界卫生组织的194个成员国发了一封信，敦促参与。
全球卫生机构还担心，现在推动疫情蔓延的是年轻人，他们中的许多人并不知道自己被感染了，这对弱势群体构成了危险。
特德罗斯推动各国加入Covax之际，欧盟、英国、瑞士和美国正在与测试未来疫苗的公司达成协议。俄罗斯和中国也在研发疫苗，世卫组织担心国家利益可能会阻碍全球努力。
“我们需要防止疫苗民族主义，”特德罗斯在一次虚拟简报会上说。“在战略上和全球范围内分享有限的供应实际上符合每个国家的国家利益。”
欧盟委员会敦促欧盟国家避开世卫组织领导的倡议，理由是担心其成本和速度。
根据路透社的统计，全球已有超过2,190万人被这种新型冠状病毒感染，772,647名​死亡。
世卫组织表示，到目前为止，COVAX基金已经吸引了92个希望自愿捐赠的较贫穷国家和80个较富裕国家的兴趣，这些国家将为该计划提供资金，与一个月前相比变化不大。
尽管如此，一些国家在做出承诺之前仍在等待8月31日的最后期限，因为该设施的条款仍在最后敲定，布鲁斯·艾尔沃德(Bruce Aylward)说，他领导着世卫组织加速新冠肺炎诊断、药物和疫苗供应的行动加速器倡议。
艾尔沃德说：“我们不是在施压让人们加入。”“我们与越来越多的玩家进行了越来越多的讨论……。克服合作可能遇到的障碍--围绕价格的问题，围绕时机的问题，以及围绕国家期望的问题。“。
世卫组织表示，目前有150多种疫苗正在开发中，约有20多种正在进行人体研究，还有几种正在进行后期试验，即使是签署双边协议的国家也通过加入Covax来提高自己的胜算。
世卫组织负责药品和疫苗获取的助理主任Mariangela Simao说：“我们还不知道哪位候选人会成功。”“通过在进行双边交易的同时加入该设施，你实际上是在押注于更多的候选疫苗。”
世卫组织仍然担心，全球年轻人的感染正在上升，使卫生系统薄弱的人口稠密地区的老年人和病人面临风险。
世卫组织西太平洋区域主任Takeshi Kasai说：“疫情正在改变。”“20多岁、30多岁和40多岁的人越来越多地推动了这一传播。”
note_en:
GENEVA/MANILA (Reuters) - Nations that hoard possible COVID-19 vaccines while excluding others will deepen the pandemic, World Health Organization (WHO) chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Tuesday, issuing a last-ditch call for countries to join a global vaccine pact.
 The WHO has an Aug. 31 deadline for wealthier nations to join the “COVAX Global Vaccines Facility” for sharing vaccine hopefuls with developing countries. Tedros said he sent a letter to the WHO’s 194 member states, urging participation.
 The global health agency also raised concerns that the pandemic’s spread was being driven now by younger people, many of whom were unaware they were infected, posing a danger to vulnerable groups.
 Tedros’ push for nations to join COVAX comes as the European Union, Britain, Switzerland and the United States strike deals with companies testing prospective vaccines. Russia and China are also working on vaccines, and the WHO fears national interests could impede global efforts.
 “We need to prevent vaccine nationalism,” Tedros told a virtual briefing. “Sharing finite supplies strategically and globally is actually in each country’s national interest.”
 The European Commission has urged EU states to skirt the WHO-led initiative, citing worries over its cost and speed.
 More than 21.9 million people have been reported to be infected by the novel coronavirus globally and 772,647​ have died, according to a Reuters tally.
  So far, the COVAX facility has attracted interest from 92 poorer countries hoping for voluntary donations and 80 wealthier countries, a number little changed from a month ago, that would finance the scheme, the WHO said.
 Still, some nations are waiting for the Aug. 31 deadline before making a commitment as the facility’s terms are still being finalised, Bruce Aylward, who leads the WHO’s ACT Accelerator initiative to speed supplies of COVID-19 diagnostics, drugs and vaccines, said.
 “We are not twisting arms for people to join,” Aylward said. “We’ve had more and more discussions with a broader and broader group of players ... to work through what might be the barriers to collaborating - issues around price, issues around timing, issues around national expectations.”
 With more than 150 vaccines in development, about two dozen in human studies and a handful in late-stage trials, the WHO said even countries signing bilateral deals boost their odds by joining COVAX.
 “Which one will be the candidate that will be successful, we don’t know yet,” said Mariangela Simao, a WHO assistant director for drug and vaccine access. “By joining the facility at the same time that you do bilateral deals, you’re actually betting on a larger number of vaccine candidates.”
   The WHO remains worried that infections among younger people are rising globally, putting at risk elderly and sick people in densely populated areas with weak health systems.
 “The epidemic is changing,” WHO Western Pacific regional director, Takeshi Kasai, said. “People in their 20s, 30s and 40s are increasingly driving the spread.”
title: 世卫组织在反对囤积的最后一搏中抨击“疫苗民族主义”
title_en: WHO Blasts 'Vaccine Nationalism' in Last-Ditch Push Against Hoarding

note:
在闭门造车的情况下，谷歌与在线旅游行业陷入了激烈的对峙。这个问题是全球性的，但德国旅游公司尤其直言不讳。
自冠状病毒大流行开始以来，活动预订平台GetYourGuide、酒店搜索网站Trivago和Airbnb的竞争对手HomeToGo一直在与这家搜索巨头就其未支付的广告费争执不下。
在线旅游公司尤其容易受到新冠肺炎疫情的破坏性经济影响，因为封锁导致全球流动性几乎陷入停滞。新的预订量枯竭，网站不得不向无法旅行的客户退还数千万美元。
在一封联名信中，一群德国旅游初创公司要求谷歌分担这一负担。多年来，谷歌通过在搜索结果中推广自己的网站来换取费用，帮助这些业务蓬勃发展。
这封信并没有像两家公司希望的那样起作用。CNBC已经能够通过多种来源和材料证实，谷歌要求的广告账单已经全额支付。
GetYourGuide首席执行长约翰·雷克(Johannes Reck)说，谷歌拒绝采取任何行动，而是在疫情肆虐之际要求我们立即付款。去年，雷克说服软银向他位于柏林的公司投资5亿美元。
雷克在接受CNBC采访时表示，谷歌在冠状病毒爆发期间的行为是引燃更大火势的火柴。
谷歌淡化了这些指控，并表示正在与旅游合作伙伴合作，帮助保护他们的业务。
该公司的一位发言人表示，我们已经采取了一系列措施，包括帮助他们在我们的旅游搜索产品中展示他们的取消政策。
随着形势变得艰难，一群德国公司在4月30日-母公司Alphabet公布季度收益、营收411亿美元的两天后-给谷歌写了一封信，要求这家搜索巨头推迟收取账单。这封信由德国初创企业协会(The Bundesverband Deutsche Startups)撰写，包括GetYourGuide和Trivago在内的八家旅游初创公司签名。
签署这封信的三家公司表示，与Facebook和微软的必应(Bing)搜索引擎不同，谷歌在推迟或减少未付账单方面并没有过度迁就。
所有签署这封信的公司都在6月或7月向谷歌全额支付了费用。
HomeToGo的联合创始人兼首席执行官帕特里克·安德雷告诉CNBC，我们对谷歌在这场持续的危机中提供的支持绝对不满意。
Trivago首席执行官阿克塞尔·赫弗(Axel Hefer)告诉CNBC，Trivago错过了谷歌的协作精神。
他补充说：作为旅游生态系统中最大的参与者之一，也可能是目前仍在盈利的极少数参与者之一，我原本预计会有更多的团结。
Facebook oﬀ让一些在线旅游公司立即推迟了60天的付款，并在复苏期间主动提供信用来测试新的广告产品，从而oﬀ弥补了部分损失。
必应立即通知ﬀ延迟付款至少90天，如果恢复不开始，正在进行审查。
GetYourGuide是对谷歌特别不满的公司之一。如果不是这家美国搜索巨头，这家初创公司的估值超过15亿美元，就不会有今天的规模。但近几个月来，两国关系变得越来越冷淡。
今年2月和3月，GetYourGuide在谷歌平台上花费了超过400万欧元(470万美元)的广告，吸引了取消订单并获得退款的客户。在此之后，预订量降至2019年水平的不到1%。
在四月的公开信之后，媒体掀起了一场风暴，雷克说谷歌关闭了几个星期。
然而，5月8日，谷歌要求GetYourGuide全额支付所有广告账单，任何取消和退款的预订都没有信用或折扣。
5月15日，谷歌高级副总裁兼首席商务官菲利普·辛德勒与在线旅游公司举行电话会议。他承诺提供慷慨的恢复方案，并承认旅行正处于可怕的困境中。该套餐包括支付支持、非Adword平台上的积分和其他福利。他还表示，谷歌也在苦苦挣扎。
之后立即没有发生任何事情。但在5月25日当周，随着德国放松了封锁措施，旅游略有回升，谷歌开始要求旅游公司承诺一项支付计划，称如果他们不遵守，他们的账户将被暂停。
当被问及可能的暂停时，谷歌的一位发言人告诉CNBC：我们对与客户或合作伙伴的付款条款是保密的，我们不能透露细节。
相反，在线旅游公司此时比以往任何时候都更需要确保谷歌搜索广告的安全。雷克说，谷歌在欧洲的搜索引擎流量占有90%的市场份额，超过75%的欧洲旅行者在旅行前咨询谷歌。RECK称，一旦GetYourGuide再次开始做广告，谷歌实质上就会说，要么立即付款，要么承诺实施这一付款计划。
付款计划允许GetYourGuide最多推迟6个月付款，但在履行付款的同时，该公司只能与谷歌做这么多广告。这说不通，因为你不想限制自己抢篮板，雷克说。
雷克和HomeToGo首席入境官多米尼克·施瓦茨(Dominik Schwarz)告诉CNBC，谷歌正试图成为旅游领域更具主导性的参与者。
可以说，这一切都始于2010年，当时谷歌收购了领先的飞行数据平台ITA。
雷克说，谷歌的旅行数据比地球上任何一家公司都要好。我们看到谷歌正积极向前发展，试图从旅游领域的其他公司获取内容，以打造自己的产品。
安德雷说，虽然业界所有公司都在争相为旅行者提供最佳体验和最佳产品，但谷歌决定免费占据整个市场的很大份额。谷歌实现这一目标的方式是，在付费结果之后，在一般搜索上实施巨大而引人注目的广告空间，免费改变流量路线，否则竞争对手就会直接进入自己的产品，根本看不到其他提供商。
欧盟执行机构欧盟委员会(European Commission)尚未决定是否调查此案，也没有发表进一步评论。
note_en:
Behind closed doors,  Google is engaged in a bitter standoff with the online travel industry. The issue is global but German travel companies have been particularly outspoken.
 Activity booking platform GetYourGuide, hotel finder  Trivago, and Airbnb rival HomeToGo have been feuding with the search giant about their unpaid advertising bills since the beginning of the coronavirus pandemic.
  Online travel companies were particularly exposed to the devastating economic impact of the Covid-19 outbreak as lockdowns brought worldwide mobility to a near standstill. New bookings dried up and the sites had to refund tens of millions of dollars to customers that were unable to travel.
 In a joint letter, a group of German travel start-ups asked Google, which has helped the businesses thrive over the years by promoting their websites in its search results in exchange for a fee, to share the burden.
 The letter didn't work as the companies hoped it would. CNBC has been able to confirm through multiple sources and materials that Google demanded advertising bills were paid in full.
 "Google refused to do anything and instead asked us to pay immediately in the midst of the pandemic," said GetYourGuide Chief Executive Johannes Reck, who persuaded SoftBank to invest $500 million in his Berlin-based company last year.
 Reck told CNBC that Google's behavior during the coronavirus "is the match that has ignited a much greater fire."
  Google played down the allegations and said it is working with its travel partners to help protect their businesses.
 "We've taken a number of measures including helping them surface their cancellation policies in our travel search products," a spokesperson for the company said.
  As times got tough, a group of German firms wrote a letter to Google on April 30 — two days after parent company  Alphabet posted quarterly earnings with $41.1 billion in revenue — asking the search giant to postpone collecting their bills. The letter was authored by the German Start-ups Association (The Bundesverband Deutsche Startups) and signed by eight travel start-ups including GetYourGuide and Trivago.
 Unlike  Facebook and  Microsoft's Bing search engine, Google has not been overly accommodating when it came to delaying or reducing unpaid bills, according to three of the companies that signed the letter.
 All of the companies that signed the letter paid Google in full in June or July.
 "We're absolutely not satisfied with the support Google offered during this ongoing crisis" Patrick Andrae, HomeToGo's co-founder and CEO, told CNBC.
 Axel Hefer, chief executive at Trivago, told CNBC that Trivago has "missed a collaborative spirit from Google."
 He added: "As one of the largest players in the travel ecosystem and probably one of the very few still generating profits right now, I would have expected more solidarity."
  Facebook oﬀered some online travel companies an immediate 60-day delay in payments and proactively provided credits to test new ad products during recovery, thereby oﬀsetting some of the incurred losses.
 Bing immediately oﬀered payment delays of at least 90 days, with ongoing review, should the recovery not start.
  GetYourGuide is one of the firms that is particularly upset with Google. Valued at over $1.5 billion, the start-up wouldn't be the size it is today if it weren't for the U.S. search giant. But the relationship has become increasingly frosty in recent months.
 In February and March, GetYourGuide spent over 4 million euros ($4.7 million) in advertising on Google's platforms, acquiring customers who canceled and were refunded. After that, bookings dropped to less than 1% of 2019 levels.
 Following April's open letter, there was a media storm, and Reck said Google "shut up for a couple of weeks."
 However, on May 8, Google asked GetYourGuide for full payment on all ad bills with no credit or discounts for any canceled and refunded bookings.
 On May 15, Philipp Schindler, senior vice president and chief business officer at Google, held a call with online travel companies. He promised a generous recovery package and acknowledged that travel is in dire straits. The package included payment support, credits on non-Adwords platforms and other benefits. He also said Google was struggling, too.
 Nothing happened immediately afterward. But in the week of May 25, as Germany eased its lockdown measures and travel picked up slightly, Google started asking travel companies to commit to a payment plan, saying their accounts would be suspended if they didn't comply.
 When asked about a possible suspension, a Google spokesperson told CNBC: "We treat payment terms with our customers or partners as confidential and we're not able to disclose details."
  Conversely, the online travel firms needed to secure Google Search ads more than ever at this time. Google has a 90% market share on search engine traffic in Europe and more than 75% of travelers in Europe consult Google before they travel, Reck said. As soon as GetYourGuide started to advertise again, Google essentially said "pay now," or commit to this payment plan, according to Reck.
 The payment plan allowed GetYourGuide to delay payments by a maximum of six months but the company would have only been able to do so much advertising with Google while fulfilling the payments. "That didn't make sense because you don't want to constrain yourself in the rebound," said Reck.
  Reck and Dominik Schwarz, chief inbound officer at HomeToGo, told CNBC that Google is trying to become a more dominant player in travel.
 Arguably, it all started in 2010, when Google bought ITA, the leading platform for flight data.
 "Google has better travel data than any other company on the planet," said Reck. "We see Google aggressively moving forward trying to get content from other companies in the travel space in order to build their own products."
  "While all companies in the industry compete over delivering the best experience and the best product for travelers, Google decided to take a big share of the whole market for free," said Andrae. "Google achieves this by implementing a huge and eye-catching advertising space on the general search after the paid results, rerouting traffic at no costs and that otherwise would go to competitors right into their own product, cutting off other providers from being seen at all."
 The European Commission, the EU's executive arm, has yet to decide whether to investigate the case and it has not offered further comment.
title: 像GetYourGuide，Trivago和HomeToGo这样遭受流行病打击的旅游公司表示，他们已经与谷歌就未付的广告费用争执了数周
title_en: Pandemic-battered travel companies like GetYourGuide, Trivago, and HomeToGo say they have been feuding with Google for weeks over outstanding ad payments

欢迎大家光临！Hacktoberfest的参与者代表151个国家和数千种独特的技能组合。这个项目欢迎所有已经加入开放源码软件社区的人，以及任何有兴趣参与其中的人。
数量是乐趣，质量是关键。参加Hacktoberfest会带来个人成长、职业机会和社区建设。然而，这一切都始于对开放源码软件的有意义的贡献。
短期行动，长期影响。在开源软件社区，我们站在前人的肩膀上。您的参与将对十月之后很久的人员和技术产生持久的影响。这是一次航行，不是比赛。
我们要求所有与会者通读细节，以确保全球社会朝着共同目标努力。感谢您尊重价值观，遵守参赛规则。
Hacktoberfest对我们全球社区的每个人开放。无论您是经验丰富的贡献者，还是正在寻找第一次参与的项目，都欢迎您参与。
拉取请求可以在任何GitHub托管的存储库/项目中进行。只要该项目是公开的，并且是由GitHub托管的，您的Pull请求就会计入您的参与。
您可以在10月1日到10月31日之间的任何时间报名。只要确保在Hacktoberfest官方网站上注册，您的拉取请求就会被计算在内。
要得到一件衬衫，你必须在10月1日至31日期间在任何时区提出四个拉扯请求(PR)。拉取请求可以发送到GitHub上的任何公共存储库，而不仅仅是突出显示的那些。拉取请求必须包含您自己做出的承诺。如果维护人员报告您的拉取请求为垃圾邮件，则不会计入您参与的Hacktoberfest。如果维护人员报告的行为不符合项目的行为准则，您将没有资格参与。今年，前75,000名参与者可以获得一件T恤。
根据Hacktoberfest的价值#2(数量有趣，质量关键)，我们提供了我们认为是低质量贡献的拉取请求示例(我们不鼓励这样做)。
自动拉入请求(例如，删除空格/修复打字错误/优化图像的脚本打开拉入请求)。
拉取具有中断性的请求(例如，获取其他人的分支/提交并发出拉取请求)。
最后但并非最不重要的一点是，一个用于修复拼写错误的Pull请求是可以的，但是删除一个零散空格的5个Pull请求就不行了。
垃圾邮件拉入请求可以被标记为无效。&#34；维护员面临着在Hacktoberfest期间发生的大多数垃圾邮件，我们和您一样不喜欢垃圾邮件。如果您是维护员，请将提交给您维护的存储库的任何垃圾邮件拉取请求标记为无效，然后将其关闭。带有此标签的拉取请求不会计入Hacktoberfest。
在完成挑战之前，所有拉取请求都有7天的审查窗口。一旦参与者提交了四个符合条件的拉式请求(准备好审核，而不是草稿)，审核窗口就开始了。这段时间让维护人员有时间识别垃圾邮件拉取请求并将其标记为无效。如果拉入请求在该窗口内未标记为无效，则它们将允许用户完成Hacktoberfest质询。如果任何拉取请求被标记为无效，则用户将返回挂起状态，直到他们有四个符合条件的拉取请求，此时审核期将再次开始。
错误的存储库将被排除。在过去，我们看到许多存储库鼓励参与者提出简单的拉取请求-例如将他们的名字添加到文件中-以快速获得完成Hacktoberfest的拉取请求。虽然这可能是新贡献者的学习工具，但它违背了我们对Hacktoberfest的核心价值观之一。拉取请求的质量是最重要的，数量次之。这些存储库不鼓励高质量的投稿，并在完成Hacktoberfest挑战方面提供了不公平的优势。我们已经实现了一个阻止这些存储库的系统，提交到这些存储库的任何拉取请求都不会被计算在内。
如果您是开放源码的新手(每个人都曾经是这样！)，您可以看一看开放源码简介系列教程。
要做出第一个贡献，需要熟悉如何创建拉取请求。
一旦您开始感觉更舒服，您可以通过以下程序找到更多需要您帮助的开放源码项目：
请查看DigitalOcean为维护人员提供的最佳实践，了解如何鼓励贡献，并在本月为自己的成功做好准备。在您的项目中创建一个好的Hacktoberfest问题的建议：
将Hacktoberfest标签贴到您的GitHub项目中准备好供新贡献者处理的问题上。
鉴于全球大流行对我们所有社区的严重影响，我们鼓励今年所有的Hacktoberfest聚会都在网上举行。我们是来帮忙的！
在10月份组织一次Hacktoberfest会议是支持和鼓励开源社区的一种很好的方式。
去年，在70个国家组织了673场活动。今年，让我们共同努力，把这个在线庆祝活动带给全世界更多的人！
浏览Hacktoberfest活动组织者工具包，了解如何在您的社区成功组织在线聚会。
对于希望增加开源项目参与度的技术公司来说，Hacktoberfest是一个很好的资源。
您的公司可以通过鼓励人们向您的存储库投稿、组织社区活动或让内部员工参与进来来参与。
Hacktoberfest官方网站上会展示参与公司的标识，以展示您对开源的支持。
我们的朋友确实在让他们的员工参与Hacktoberfest方面取得了很大的成功。他们在DevRelCon上谈到了这一点，并正在创建一个案例研究来帮助您的公司做到这一点。[马上就来！]。
SendGrid还撰写了这份指南，概述了他们如何利用Hacktoberfest将他们的开源贡献增加了3510%，并谈到了他们在DevRelCon的增长。
note_en:
Everyone is welcome! Participants in Hacktoberfest represented 151 countries and thousands of unique skill sets. This program welcomes everyone already who’s already part of the open source software community, and anyone who is interested in diving in.
 Quantity is fun, quality is key. Participating in Hacktoberfest leads to personal growth, professional opportunities, and community building. However, it all begins with meaningful contributions to open source software.
 Short-term action, long-term impact. In the open source software community, we are standing on the shoulders of those who came before us. Your participation has a lasting effect on people and technology long after October. This is a voyage not a race.
  We ask all participants to read through the details to ensure that the global community is working toward a shared goal. Thank you for honoring the values and following the rules of participation.
   Hacktoberfest is open to everyone in our global community. Whether you’re a seasoned contributor or looking for projects to contribute to for the first time, you’re welcome to participate.
 Pull requests can be made in any GitHub-hosted repositories/projects. As long as the project is public and GitHub-hosted, your pull requests will count toward your participation.
 You can sign up anytime between October 1 and October 31. Just be sure to sign up on the official Hacktoberfest website for your pull requests to count.
   To get a shirt, you must make four pull requests (PRs) between October 1–31 in any time zone. Pull requests can be to any public repository on GitHub, not just the ones highlighted. The pull request must contain commits you made yourself. If a maintainer reports your pull request as spam, it will not be counted toward your participation in Hacktoberfest. If a maintainer reports behavior that’s not in line with the project’s code of conduct, you will be ineligible to participate. This year, the first 75,000 participants can earn a T-shirt.
   In line with Hacktoberfest value #2 (Quantity is fun, quality is key), we have provided examples of pull requests that we consider to be low-quality contributions (which we discourage).
 Pull requests that are automated (e.g. scripted opening pull requests to remove whitespace/fix typos/optimize images).
 Pull requests that are disruptive (e.g. taking someone else's branch/commits and making a pull request).
 Last but not least, one pull request to fix a typo is fine, but 5 pull requests to remove a stray whitespace is not.
 Spammy pull requests can be labeled as "invalid." Maintainers are faced with the majority of spam that occurs during Hacktoberfest, and we dislike spam just as much as you. If you're a maintainer, please label any spammy pull requests submitted to the repositories you maintain as invalid, and close them. Pull requests with this label won't count toward Hacktoberfest.
 There's a seven-day review window for all pull requests before they count toward completing the challenge. Once a participant has submitted four eligible pull requests (ready-to-review, not drafts), the review window begins. This period gives maintainers time to identify and label spammy pull requests as invalid. If the pull requests are not marked as invalid within that window, they will allow the user to complete the Hacktoberfest challenge. If any of the pull requests are labeled as invalid, the user will return to the pending state until they have four eligible pull requests, at which point the review period will start again.
 Bad repositories will be excluded. In the past, we've seen many repositories that encourage participants to make simple pull requests – such as adding their name to a file – to quickly gain a pull request toward completing Hacktoberfest. While this may be a learning tool for new contributors, it goes against one of our core values for Hacktoberfest. The quality of pull requests is paramount; quantity comes second. These repositories do not encourage quality contributions and provide an unfair advantage in completing the Hacktoberfest challenge. We've implemented a system to block these repositories, and any pull requests submitted to such repositories will not be counted.
 If you’re new to open source (which everyone was once!), you can take a look at the  Introduction to Open Source tutorial series.
 To make your first contribution, it’s required to familiarize yourself with how to create a pull request.
   Once you start feeling more comfortable, you can find more open source projects that can use your help through the following programs:
       Check out DigitalOcean’s  best practices for maintainers to learn how to encourage contributions and set yourself up for success this month. Recommendations for creating a good Hacktoberfest issue in your project:
 Apply the "Hacktoberfest" label to issues in your GitHub project that are ready for new contributors to work on.
     Given the severe impact that the global pandemic has had on all of our communities, we encourage all Hacktoberfest meetups this year to happen online. We’re here to help!
 Organizing a Hacktoberfest meetup in October is a great way to support and encourage the open source community.
  Last year, 673 events were organized in 70 countries. This year, let’s work together to bring this celebration online to even more people around the world!
 Explore the  Hacktoberfest Event Organizer Kit to learn how to successfully organize an online gathering in your community.
      Hacktoberfest is a great resource for technology companies looking to increase participation in their open source projects.
 Your company can participate by encouraging people to contribute to your repositories, organizing community events, or engaging internal employees.
 Logos from participating companies are displayed on the official Hacktoberfest website to showcase your support of open source.
 Our friends at Indeed had a lot of success getting their employees involved in Hacktoberfest. They’ve  talked about this at DevRelCon and are creating a case study to help your company do the same. [Coming soon!]
 SendGrid also wrote  this guide outlining how they used Hacktoberfest to  increase their open source contributions by 3,510%, and spoke  about their growth at DevRelCon.
title: 2020年Hacktoberfest！耶！

地球上的生命是起源于地球，还是起源于火星并迁移到地球？如果你把这种种子病定义为“非凡的要求”，要求“非凡的证据”，你当然会得出结论，认为这应该被“怀疑”为不太可能的、草率的、不科学的“推测”。被受人尊敬的学者和科学记者鄙视和不严肃对待。但这真的不公平。
你看，从先验的角度看，火星和地球一样可能是生命起源的地方。因此，如果生命在两个行星之间转移的速率足够高，那么首先在两个地方开始生命的机会相等，那么地球生命在任何一个地方开始的机会都是相等的。我们应该考虑两个地方生命开始之间的预期时差，并询问在这段时间内生命从一个星球移动到另一个星球的可能性有多高。岩石从一个地方扔到另一个地方的频率越高，生命就越容易在这些岩石内的旅行期间存活下来，那么地球生命就越有可能起源于火星。
此外，火星距离太阳较远，将首先冷却，并在生命之窗先行一步。这使得生命更有可能从那里开始并传播到地球，而不是反之亦然。当然，首先在火星上开始的生命会对我们观察火星时可能看到的东西产生影响。如果我们曾预期火星生命会持续到今天，那么我们现在在火星上看不到生命的事实将是对这一假设的重大打击。但是，如果我们预计火星生命到现在已经灭绝或至少进入休眠状态，那么这意味着我们在火星上挖掘时会看到什么。有了这样的挖掘的足够数据，我们可能会拒绝接受火星优先假设，即使它最初是可信的。
类似的分析也适用于来自其他恒星的受精症。你可能会认为很明显，一颗恒星充满生命的岩石向其他恒星播种的速率非常低，但大多数恒星都是在恒星托儿所中紧密相连的大群中诞生的。因此，如果生命在我们的恒星的苗圃中出现得足够早，那么在那个苗圃的恒星之间可能会有很高的移动率。在这种情况下，地球生命来自另一颗恒星的可能性也很高，在我们的恒星之外寻找生命的最佳地点将是我们恒星苗圃中的其他恒星。
现在来考虑一下先进文明失落的说法。不仅仅是在空间和时间上与周围文明处于相似发展水平的文明；考虑到我们一直在寻找新的定居点和发达的地方，这是很有可能的。不，更有趣的说法是关于大范围文明峰值或中位数水平的下降。比如地中海青铜时代晚期或罗马帝国灭亡时发生的事情。在我们现在所知的每个地区的“第一个”文明之前，有没有可能有“更高”的文明，如苏美尔人、埃及人和中国商朝？(我说的是人类文明，不是其他文明。)。
是的，你可能会认为这些是我们缺乏非凡证据的“非同寻常”的主张，并宣称它们不太可能，不太可能是草率的、不科学的猜测，值得尊敬的人不屑一顾。但是再说一次，这是不公平的。从先验上看，一个地区的整体进步可能会出现暂时的下滑，就像它会跟随最近通常的上涨一样。不，这不是一个值得怀疑的理由。
一些支持性的证据来自我们能找到的最早的作者的著作，他们明确地声称他们是更先进的先前文明的后裔，这些文明由于大灾难而衰落。这个故事其实很常见。当我们看到的最初文明的最早版本在关键领域拥有令人惊讶的先进能力时，更多的支持证据被发现，这些能力随后随着时间的推移而下降。这就是你在之前的峰值之后会看到的情况。据我所知，这似乎是我们在埃及和秘鲁看到的，关于石头砌筑能力。当然，这也可能只是反映了特定能力的局部波动；最大的问题是，不同类型的文明能力之间有多大的相关性。
最常见的相反证据是缺乏预期的支持证据。例如：
不管外星人的影响可能有多大的破坏性，我们能相信在经历了几个世纪的繁荣之后，每一件最后的工具、陶片、衣物，大概还有来自先进文明的文字、冶金和其他技术-更不用说垃圾-都被抹去了吗？难以想象。(更多信息)。
他声称，彗星焚烧覆盖北美的冰原产生的冰川径流可能摧毁了文明的每一丝痕迹，尽管动物骨骼是如何幸存下来的，但没有一块石头或金属工具，也没有一块无可争辩的人类雕刻的石块，这让我无法理解…。克洛维斯人留下了数以万计的石器和凹槽，而亚特兰蒂斯则什么都没有留下。他们的骨头即使化为尘土，他们的石头和金属在哪里呢？他们农作物的花粉在哪里？…。？(更多信息)。
这里的关键问题是：如果它们真的在那里，你应该期待已经看到的东西是什么？一方面，我们显然已经看到了足够多的证据，可以安全地得出结论，在我们的主要城市中心，没有大型恐龙在街道上漫步。另一方面，我们经常听到报告说，人们发现了别人曾自信地预测永远找不到的旧东西。这使得许多人怀疑，人们普遍过度自信地声称我们知道的东西不可能在那里，因为如果是这样的话，我们早就已经看到了。
是的，一个人假设的文明规模越大，技术含量越高，我们就越有可能看到它的证据。例如，他们越早采用陶器，越广泛地使用陶器，我们就越有可能发现陶器碎片。类似于金属工具的广泛使用。但是，如果有看似合理的CIV假设，不要求它们有那么大的规模，或者对创造长期证据的东西有同样多的兴趣，那么我们拒绝这些假设的麻烦就越大。
重新失去先进文明的一个复杂因素是，在过去的7K年里，世界各地的天气特别平静。在此之前，海平面变化更大，在10K之前，气温变化更大，地球上大部分地区都被冰川覆盖。甚至可能在12Kya附近发生了一些巨大的世界性大灾难。所有这些都使得当时维持复杂的文明变得更加困难，但也使得保存它们的证据让我们现在更难看到，如果它们曾经在那里的话。
在我看来，我们想要一些像预测市场这样的东西，来提供更好的激励和聚合重新预测什么东西会在什么时候在哪里找到。所以让我建议：考古学的市场奖励义务。
首先，让我们设立一些考古学奖品，每个奖品支付P美元给第一组，他们可以展示在D日期之前在R地区发现的X。例如，展示在200Kya之前在美国发现的智人头骨。用一些标准投资资产的单位来定义$P，比如标准普尔5000指数或摩根士丹利资本国际全球指数(MSCI All Country World Index)。然后创造市场，在那里人们可以在这些相同的单位获得报酬，承担一小部分奖金义务。例如，某人可能会被支付10个单位，以承担支付200 Kya之前的美国骨骼奖中的100个单位的义务。
那些认为戏剧性发现的可能性很小的怀疑者可能会热切地获得报酬，让他们承担这样的义务，而认为这样的发现更有可能的狂热者可能会采取与此类交易相反的立场。当世界意识到并同意他们的看法时，双方都可以通过逆转交易来获利，这可能发生在任何实际发现获得奖励之前很久。此外，希望很快能够展示特定发现的调查人员可能会提出现在就为其他人支付费用，让他们承担以后支付更大相关奖金的义务。
这些市场的资产比率价格，例如最后一个例子中10比100的10%比率，可以被解释为有朝一日中奖的可能性。有了足够多的奖品来获得足够的发现X、地区R和日期D，我们就可以对我们可能找到的东西有一个相当好的了解。比如失落的先进文明。这些奖金将鼓励更多的考古学努力去发现东西。
由于支付奖金和转移支付奖金的义务是相当合法的，所以在我看来，所有这些都是合法的。(但我不是律师，所以法律上当然不允许对这类事情发表意见。)。当然，你需要设置一些东西，以确保人们履行支付奖金的义务，但这似乎是可行的。
note_en:
Did life on Earth start on Earth, or did it start on Mars and move to Earth? If you frame such panspermia as an “extraordinary claim” for which you demand “extraordinary evidence”, you will of course conclude that this should be treated “skeptically” as unlikely and sloppy unscientific “speculation”. To be disdained and not treated as serious by respectable academics and science journalists. But that’s not really fair.
 You see Mars is, a priori, just as likely a place for life to start as Earth. So if the rate at which life is transferred between the planets were high enough, then equal chances of life starting first in both places would results in equal chances for Earth life to have started in either place. We should take the expected time difference between life starting in the two places, and ask how high is the chance that life would move from one planet to the next during that period. The more often rocks are thrown from one place to the other, and the more easily life could survive for the travel period within those rocks, then the more likely it is that Earth life started on Mars.
 In addition, Mars, being further from the Sun, would have cooled first, and had a head start in its window for life. Making it  more likely that life would start there and spread to Earth than vice versa. Of course life starting first on Mars would have implications for what we might see when we look at Mars. If we had expected Mars life to continue strong until today, then the fact that we see no life on Mars now would be a big strike against this hypothesis. But if we expected Mars life to have died out or at least gone dormant by now, then the implications are about what we will see when we dig on Mars. With enough data on such digs, we might then come to reject to Mars first hypothesis even given its initial plausibility.
 A similar analysis applies to panspermia from other stars. You might think it obvious that the rate at which life-filled rocks from a star make it to seed other stars is very low, but most stars are born in large groups close together in stellar nurseries. So if life arose early enough within our star’s nursery, there might have been high rates of moving that life between stars in that nursery. In which  case the chance that Earth life came from another star could also be high, and the best place to look for life outside our star would be the other stars from our stellar nursery.
 Now consider claims of lost advanced civilizations. Not just civilizations at a similar level of development to those around them in space and time; that’s quite likely given that we keep finding new settlements and developed places. No, the more interesting claims are about decreases in the peak or median level of civilizations across wide areas. Such as what happened late in the Mediterranean  Bronze Age, or at the fall of the Roman Empire. Could there have been “higher” civilizations before the “first” ones that we now know about in each region, such as the Sumerians, Egyptians, and Chinese Shang dynasty? (I’m talking human civs,  not  others.)
 Yes, you might think of these as “extraordinary” claims for which we lack extraordinary evidence, and declare them unlikely and sloppy unscientific speculation, to be disdained by the respectable. But again, that’s not fair. A priori it is nearly as likely that overall advancement in a region would have taken a temporary dip, as that it would have followed its recently-usual rise. No, that isn’t much of a reason for skepticism.
 Some supporting evidence comes in the form of writings from the earliest authors we can find, who explicitly claim that they descended from more advanced prior civilizations, who fell due to big cataclysms. This story is actually quite common. More supporting evidence is found when the earliest versions of the first civilizations we see had surprisingly advanced abilities for their time in key areas, abilities which then declined over time. That is what you’d expect to see after a prior peak. And that does seem to be what we  see in Egypt and Peru, as far as I can tell, regarding stone masonry abilities. Of course that might also just reflect local fluctuations in particular abilities; the big question is how much correlation to expect to see across different kinds of civilization abilities.
 The most common contrary evidence offered is the absence of expected supporting evidence. For example:
 No matter how devastating an extraterrestrial impact might be, are we to believe that after centuries of flourishing every last tool, potsherd, article of clothing, and, presumably from an advanced civilization, writing, metallurgy and other technologies—not to mention trash—was erased? Inconceivable. ( More)
 He claims that glacial runoff from the comet’s incineration of the ice sheets covering North America could have destroyed every trace of civilization, though how animal bones survived but not a single stone or metal tool, or a single indisputably human-carved block of stone is beyond me … Clovis people left behind tens of thousands of stone tools and fluted points, while Atlantis is represented by exactly nothing. Even if their bones turned to dust, where are their stones and their metals? Where is the pollen from their crops…? ( More)
 Here the key question here is: what sort of things should you expect to have already seen, if they were really there? On the one hand, we clearly have seen enough to safely conclude that there aren’t large dinosaurs roaming the streets in our major city centers. On the other hand, we  often  hear  reports of people uncovering old things that others had confidently predicted would never be found. Which makes many suspect widespread overconfidence in claims about what we know can’t be there, because if so we would have seen them already.
 Yes, the bigger and more techy a civilization one postulates, the more likely it is that we’d have seen evidence of it. For example, the earlier they adopted pottery, and the more widely they used it, the more we should expect to find pottery shards. Similar for widespread use of metal tools. But if there are plausible civ hypotheses that don’t require them to be as big, or as much into stuff that creates long-lasting evidence, then the more trouble we’ll have rejecting such hypotheses.
 One complication re lost advanced civilizations is that the last 7K years have seen especially calm weather worldwide. Before that, sea levels  changed a lot more, and before 10K temperatures  changed a lot more, and much of the Earth was covered with glaciers. There may even have been some huge worldwide cataclysms  around 12Kya. All this made it harder to sustain complex civilizations back then, but also made it harder to preserve evidence of them for us to see now, if they has been there.
 Seems to me we want something like prediction markets here, to give better incentives and aggregation re predictions of what stuff will be found where re when. So let me suggest: markets in archeology prize obligations.
 First, let’s set up some archeology prizes, each of which pays $P to the first group who can show an X found in region R from before date D. For example, show a homo sapien skull found in the Americans dated before 200Kya. Define $P in units of some standard investment asset, like the S&P 5000 or MSCI All Country World Index. Then create markets where people can be paid in those same units to take on fractions of prize obligations. For example, someone might be paid 10 units to take on an obligation to pay 100 units of the pre-200Kya America skeleton prize.
 Skeptics who see little chance of dramatic findings might eagerly be paid to take on such obligations, while enthusiasts who see such discoveries as more likely could take the opposite sides of such transactions. Each side can expect to profit by reversing their trade when the world comes to its senses and agrees with them, which might happen long before any actual discoveries are paid prizes. Also, investigators who expect to be able to show particular findings soon might offer to pay now for others to take on obligations to pay bigger related prizes later.
 The asset ratio price in these markets, such as the 10% ratio of 10 to 100 in this last example, could be interpreted as a probability that the prize will ever be won. With enough kinds of prizes for enough findings X, regions R, and dates D, we could get a pretty good picture of what we are likely to find. Such as lost advanced civilizations. These prize payments would encourage more archeology effort to discover things.
 And as it is quite legal to pay out prizes and to transfer obligations to pay prizes, all of this looks pretty legal to me. (But I’m not a lawyer, so of course not legally allowed to state opinions on such things.) Of course you’d need to set things up to ensure that people will make good on obligations to pay prizes, but that seems feasible.
title: 失落的先进文明

这项投资与我们为印度每个人提供数字接入的承诺相一致。Netmeds的加入增强了Reliance Retail提供高质量和负担得起的保健产品和服务的能力，还扩大了其数字商务主张，将消费者的大多数日常必需品包括在内。“Reliance Retail董事Isha Ambani在一份声明中说。
信实以620克朗收购Netmeds(60%)。这很有趣-请记住，Reliance是印度人，可以有库存。我真的很喜欢这个--这个游戏真的很有规模。披露：投资于Reliance。
信实零售与其姊妹电信企业Jio Platforms一样，是印度估值最高的公司信实工业(Reliance Industries)的子公司。信实工业由亚洲首富穆凯什·安巴尼(Mukesh Ambani)经营。
在周二晚间(当地时间)宣布这一消息的几天前，亚马逊与NetMeds、1 mg、PharmEasy和Medlife达成了在班加罗尔在线销售药品的协议。该公司表示，这是亚马逊首次扩展到这一类别。分析人士说，冠状病毒大流行加速了远程医疗和制药市场在该国的采用。
新德里目前还没有对印度的在线药品销售进行明确的监管，这给信实零售带来了另一个重大机遇。最近几个季度，信实零售将其新的电子商务企业JioMart扩大到了200多个城镇。
据当地媒体报道，Reliance还在洽谈收购在线家具店Urban梯子、送奶初创公司MilkBasket和总部位于班加罗尔的内衣制造商Zivame。TechCrunch上周报道称，信实工业也在就收购TikTok的印度业务进行谈判。
note_en:
The all-cash deal valued  Netmeds, which serves 5.7 million customers in more than 670 cities and towns and online, at about $134 million. Consumers get access to more than 70,000 prescription drugs for chronic and recurring ailments as well as enhanced lifestyle drugs and thousands of non-prescription goods for wellness, health, and personal care through Netmeds.
 “This investment is aligned with our commitment to provide digital access for everyone in India. The addition of Netmeds enhances Reliance Retail’s ability to provide good quality and affordable health care products and services, and also broadens its digital commerce proposition to include most daily essential needs of consumers,” said Isha Ambani, Director of Reliance Retail, in a statement.
  Reliance buys Netmeds (60%) for 620 cr. This is interesting – remember, Reliance is Indian and can have inventory. I really like this – the game is really one of scale. Disclosure: Invested in Reliance
 Reliance Retail, like its sister telecom venture Jio Platforms, is a subsidiary of Reliance Industries, the most valued firm in India. Reliance Industries is run by Mukesh Ambani, Asia’s richest man.
 The announcement late Tuesday evening (local time) comes days after  Amazon   struck a deal with NetMeds, 1mg, PharmEasy and Medlife to  sell medicines online in Bangalore. It was the first time Amazon had expanded into this category, it said. The coronavirus pandemic has accelerated the adoption of telemedicine and pharma marketplaces in the country, analysts said.
  Online sales of medicine in India, for which New Delhi currently does not have clear regulations, presents yet another major opportunity for Reliance Retail, which has expanded its new e-commerce venture — called JioMart — to  more than 200 cities and towns in the recent quarters.
 Local media has reported that Reliance is also in talks to acquire online furniture store Urban Ladder, milk delivery startup MilkBasket and Bangalore-based lingerie maker Zivame. TechCrunch reported last week that Reliance Industries was also in talks to  acquire the India business of TikTok.
title: 印度Reliance Retail以8320万美元收购在线药房Nedmeds的多数股权
title_en: India’s Reliance Retail acquires a majority stake in online pharmacy Nedmeds for $83.2M

note:
这款代号为“我的第一款ModelY”的产品是特斯拉设计工作室和Radio Flyer的产品团队共同努力的成果。这是一个搭便车版本，而标准的Model Y并非如此，它包括一个按喇叭，以及黑色感应轮(在真实汽车上的升级选项)和一个功能性方向盘，售价为99美元。只有一个修剪级别。
与Radio Flyer和特斯拉(Tesla Model S For Kids)的首次合作不同，这款车没有内置电池-它需要儿童电源才能工作。这意味着更多的负担能力，并使其适合更小的孩子。
我可能会拿起其中的一个，而不是继续用油腻的铅笔在我1998年的丰田凯美瑞(Toyota Camry)的后窗上用大写字母写着“特斯拉”(Tesla)。
note_en:
Dubbed ‘My First Model Y,’ this is a collaborative effort between the Tesla Design Studio and  Radio Flyer’s   product team. It’s a ride-on version, which is not true of the standard Model Y, and it includes a honking horn, as well as black induction wheels (an upgrade option on the real car) and a functional steering wheel, with a price point of $99. There’s only one trim level.
   Unlike the first collaboration between Radio Flyer and Tesla, the Tesla Model S for Kids, this one doesn’t have a built-in battery – it requires kid power to function. That means a lot more affordability, and makes it suitable for much younger kids.
 I might pick up one of these instead of just continuing to scrawl “Tesla” in block letters on the rear window of my 1998 Toyota Camry in grease pencil.
title: Radio Flyer与特斯拉联手推出一款儿童大小的特斯拉Model Y
title_en: Radio Flyer teams up with Tesla to launch a tyke-sized Tesla Model Y

note:
UnitedMaster是一家非唱片公司，实际上是由Interscope唱片公司前总裁史蒂夫·斯托特创立的直接发行公司。该公司允许音乐家(特别是初出茅庐的音乐家)支付具有竞争力的发行费，以获得Spotify、YouTube、SoundCloud、Apple Music和其他服务。它还让他们能够访问UM与ESPN和NBA等品牌达成的分析、重新定位、CRM工具和个人交易。
通常，从艺人能够在TikTok上走红，到进入下一场NBA2k，或者在正式比赛播出之前，这条路会很长，需要付出很多代价才能分到一块蛋糕。UnitedMaster为这一切提供了捷径。
一位有抱负的艺术家或词曲作者在TikTok上发布了一首歌曲或即兴小品(很可能是众多歌曲中的一个)。
他们放弃10%的分成，但直接分销到主要的流媒体桶和潜在的A级合作伙伴中。(还有5美元/月的订阅选项。)。
他们还可以使用UM的客户工具向粉丝推销门票、集市和更直接的东西。
这就是为什么与TikTok的合作是非常有意义的地狱。病毒式社交平台最大的问题之一就是奖励创作者的方式。当然，Twitter的Vine在那里浪费了机会。即使是YouTube也在向许多顶级创作者提供持续的收入方面遇到了重大问题，长期以来，大红大紫的人为了赚取稳定、持久的收入而在平台外货币化的趋势由来已久。
TikTok已经宣布了一项资金雄厚的创客基金，但它还需要更多的资金。我们已经一次又一次地看到平台上的年轻创作者如何为TikTok以及数百万的重现和混音创造病毒式的关注浪潮。然而，通常情况下，这些创作者几乎没有获得货币化或从他们的创作中受益的追索权。舞蹈创作者和音乐天才，通常是年轻的黑人女性，都在TikTok上实时制作文化，她们获得物质利益的途径非常罕见。当然，当一名发起人被一名愿意追查源头的时报记者叫出来时，这是很棒的，但每个月都有数以千计的其他人在这个平台上被塑造成真正的声音呢？
对于文化的创造者来说，从那种文化中获益已经是时不我待了。这就是为什么我觉得UnitedMaster的交易如此有趣。向观众提供一条直接的渠道，而不是唱片行业设备随之而来的秃鹫主义，真的与TikTok这样的平台很好地结合在一起，TikTok通过合作表演来鼓励和启用“病毒式声音”。传统的交易结构不太适合捕捉病毒式炒作，在没有额外燃料的情况下，这种炒作可以在几周内起伏不定。
就整体平台而言，TikTok显然拥有市场上令人难以置信的、尚未开发的音乐人才的最高集中度。我在那里看到的创作者即使不比你在收音机里听到的好，也是一样好，真是太疯狂了。歌剧，说唱，灵魂，民谣，喜剧，歌曲创作，它涵盖了所有的领域。
TikTok首席执行官凯文·梅耶尔在迪士尼工作了很长一段时间后来到公司，以非常成功的迪士尼+发布告终。几乎立刻，他就陷入了中美两国政府之间的政治风暴。特朗普表示，母公司ByteDance必须在90天内出售，否则将被关闭。微软可能会收购它们。其他科技公司也在观望。这笔交易是一个相当明确的前瞻性信号，表明TikTok看到了解决这一问题的方法，不会等待在这个用户生成业务时代的更棘手的组件上进行创新。
最重要的是，它为用户生成的平台应该如何看待服务创建者并让他们留在自己的世界中绘制了一条路线。所有的UGC游戏都从用户的创造性能量中获得了显著的价值，但很少有人找到一种方法，让这种关系以一种感觉可持续的方式互惠互利。
UnitedMaster的这笔交易感觉不同，也是一个更大趋势的开始，可能会给平台和最终的创造者带来巨大的红利。
note_en:
UnitedMasters   is the un-record-label label — in fact a  direct distribution company founded by former president of Interscope Records, Steve Stoute. The firm allows musicians (especially budding ones) to pay a competitive distribution rate to get access to Spotify, YouTube, SoundCloud, Apple Music and other services. It also gets them access to analytics, retargeting, CRM tools and individual deals that UM makes with brands like ESPN and the NBA.
 Normally, the path between an artist being able to go viral on TikTok and be included in the next  NBA   2k or before an official game on the air would be a long one involving a lot of knives out for pieces of the pie. UnitedMasters shortcuts all of this.
   An aspiring artist or songwriter puts out a song or riff on TikTok (likely one of many).
   They give up a cut of 10% but get direct distribution into the major streaming buckets and potential A-grade partners. (There’s also a $5/mo  subscription option.)
 They can also market things like tickets, merch and more directly to fans using UM’s customer tools.
 Which is why a tie up with TikTok makes a hell of a lot of sense. One of the biggest issues with viral social platforms has been the way that they reward creators. Twitter’s Vine, of course, squandered their  opportunity there. Even YouTube has had major problems providing consistent revenue to many of its top creators, with a long trend towards big hitters monetizing off platform in order to earn consistent, durable money.
 TikTok has already  announced a creators fund with a significant purse, but it needs to go beyond that. We’ve seen over and over how young creators on the platform create viral waves of attention for TikTok and millions of re-enactments and remixes. Often, though, those creators are offered little recourse to monetize or benefit from their creations. Dance creators and musical talents, often young Black women, are literally crafting culture in real-time on TikTok and the pathways for them to benefit materially are very rare. Sure, it’s great when an originator gets called out by a  Times reporter willing to do the work to trace the source, but what about the thousands of others being minted as a real voice on the platform every month?
 It’s beyond time for the creators of The Culture to benefit from that culture. That’s why I find this UnitedMasters deal so interesting. Offering a direct pipeline to audiences without the attendant vulture-ism of the recording industry apparatus is really well aligned with a platform like TikTok, which encourages and enables ‘viral sounds’ with collaborative performances. Traditional deal structures are not well suited to capturing viral hype, which can rise and fall within weeks without additional fuel.
 In terms of overall platforms, TikTok clearly has the highest concentration of incredible and un-tapped musical talent on the market. It’s just wild how many creators I see on there that are just flat out as good if not better than what you hear on the radio. Opera, rap, soul, folk, comedy, songwriting, it runs the gamut.
 TikTok CEO  Kevin Mayer   came to the company after a long stint at Disney ending with a very successful Disney+ launch. Almost immediately, he was dropped into a political firestorm between China and the U.S. government. Parent company  ByteDance   must sell within 90 days, says Trump, or get shut down. Microsoft might buy them. Other tech companies are circling. This deal is a pretty crisp forward-looking signal that TikTok sees a way through this and is not waiting to innovate on one of the trickier components of this era of user generated businesses.
 And on top of that, it charts a course for how user generated platforms should look to service creators and keep them in their universe. All UGC plays garner significant value from the creative energies of their users, but few have found a way to make that relationship reciprocal in a way that feels sustainable.
 This UnitedMasters deal feels different, and the start of a larger trend that could pay big dividends to platforms and, finally, creators.
title: TikTok的UnitedMaster大交易是创作者寻求保护他们的包的前进方向
title_en: TikTok’s big UnitedMasters deal is the way forward for creators looking to secure their bag

note_en:
Getting new users is important, but no acquisition efforts will last if you don't give your users something to stay for. In this article I'll argue its much more effective to engage your users with drip email campaigns that get triggered from a specific user action, and I'll show you how to create your own automated drip campaigns based on what your customers are doing with  Palabra.
  If you have a subscription-based business, you are well aware of how important engagement is for your monthly revenue. But no matter what type of product or service you have, having people keep interacting with your site or app is probably really good for business.
 Getting a new customer can be  7 times more expensive than getting new sales from your existing contact-base. Focusing on retention also enables you to create long-lasting relationships with your customers, and can be part of your customer experience added value.
 Personally, I like focusing on retention because I love talking to users and learning from them. When people respond to our ongoing sequences, I have a much better understanding of what their problems are, and that gives me valuable information to:
 Optimize our acquisition strategy, by telling me who they are and how I can find more users like them.
 Define our product roadmap, by letting us know what they would like Palabra to do, instead of just deciding on new features they probably won't need.
  As the name suggests, drip email campaigns are sequences of emails sent to get users to a specific goal drip by drip during a certain period of time.
 Drip campaigns can vary in terms of frequency and duration, but they all serve the purpose of nurturing users or prospects without overwhelming them with ever-lasting emails.
 A well used drip campaign has much higher read and click rates than promotional emails — up to 3 times higher than single send emails. It takes a bit of work to create a good drip campaign, but the results will very much be worth your time.
 There are three things to consider while setting up drip sequences to make them work:
    To create your own drip campaigns for retention, I suggest a data-driven approach: have a clear goal of what you want to achieve with them first, and create the emails last. Defining your strategy before creating your content will save you a lot of time later, since it will give you a clear idea of the content you should be sending.
 When it comes to retention, the purpose of drip sequences can vary depending on what retention means for your business. For e-commerces for example, retaining customers means offering more products to the same customers, based on their previous preferences.
 For a subscription-based tool like  Palabra, our retention goals for first time users are tw:
 Getting first time users to automate their first campaign. People usually come to Palabra with a specific problem in mind, they are trying to automate something and are looking for the easiest way to do it. The thing is, they usually don't do it as soon as they create their account. We need to remind them to complete their first sequence in order to start seeing results.
 Getting users to automate more sequences: Once they solve their initial problem, we want them to keep automating triggered emails. We usually tailor use cases based on what triggers they used (what integration they chose) and the type of business they have.
 If you want to use drip campaigns to improve your retention rates, you first need to find a specific goal you want your users to achieve. These goals will be specific to your business and user base, but they need to be very clear. "Getting people to stay around" is too vague.
  What's the main action you want your users to achieve? What problem does your product solve?
 I used  Amplitude's guide to create Palabra's retention goals, I recommend it if you're starting to think about retention for your product. We also use their tool to measure and optimize our retention strategies.
  Once you have your retention strategy defined, you can set up a drip campaign to get users to continue along your user journey.
 The first thing to keep in mind while designing a drip campaign is timing. How long does it usually take your users to complete the key action you're aiming for?
 When we created our first onboarding sequence in Palabra, we didn't really know how long it would take people to create a trigger. So we created our first onboarding sequence just thinking about what we thought would make sense, and ended up with a simple drip of 3 onboarding emails.
  After getting our first 20 or so active users, we found out  it takes people about two weeks to set up their first trigger. We found that people take time to clean up their strategies after creating their account, even if they have a specific problem in mind that they want to solve.
 We were really surprised to learn that, since it takes no more than 20 minutes to actually create a trigger, and only  5 minutes to connect Palabra to Google Sheets. But real users don't lie, so we changed our onboarding sequence.
 Instead of offering help just 2 days later, we wait for a week, to give them time to look around first. And after they do turn their first trigger, we show them other things they can do with our platform, offering them to complete challenges
  There is no recipe to create drip campaigns that will work for everyone. The best thing that you can do is get to talking to your users as early as possible, and learn from what they're actually doing with your product.
 After you identify the timing behind each action they make, it will be much easier to set up different emails that lead users to solve their own problems.
 Keep in mind what your users are actually doing and not what you would like for them to do. If you don't have users yet, take your best guess but make sure to improve them after you have real data.
  Most drip campaigns are triggered when a person moves through the conversion funnel. Those are usually actions they take to subscribe or buy something. That could be just enough to create your first automated drip sequence, and it's easy enough to start.
 One of those examples is the most common drip campaign for retention: the onboarding sequence. They tipically consist on a few emails showing a few interesting features that may be helpful, offering help and asking for feedback.  Superhuman has a great onboarding drip that consist of one email per day during the first week to show the basic features, and then some tricks or features 2 times a week afterwards.
 To automate an onboarding sequence, you have to set up a trigger based on subscription (or account creation). To do that with  Palabra you can choose from many integrations depending on your stack, for example if you have a Webflow landing page, you can send an  automated sequence each time someone submits a form to subscribe.
  To engage retention from other user actions, you should first think about how often you would need to send those emails before automating. It may be easier to send emails manually yourself if you have 10 or less users that complete those actions each week.
 In most self-service products, automation is almost always the best choice. Having a fully automated flow can save you a lot of time once your users start to grow.
 For example, if you are selling courses online, you may want to create a short sequence that starts once they complete the course, and show them what other things they can learn on your site. Most online learning platforms (and many other products) connect to Zapier, so you can set up a Zapier trigger to Palabra if you want to  automatically start a sequence to re-engage them.
  To engage users there's no work-for-all recipe. You have to actually take time to find out what it is your users are doing and how you can help them move on to their next interaction with your product. Once you know that and have a clear goal in mind, drip sequences will help you educate and nurture your users without overwhelming them with long emails.
 To use drip sequences for retention and save some precious time, you can choose a tool to automate sequences triggered by user actions. Just find out what action can lead to the next one and start with simple sequences to engage your users.
 As an early stage product, we are constantly improving our sequences and retention strategies, so I know how time-consuming it can be. My recommendation is that you choose a tool that you're familiar with or that has a short learning curve to make your first automations. If it takes too much time to learn, automating can take you more time than it saves.
 If you have questions or want to share what drip sequences you're automating to drive retention, just  send me an email or ping us at  Twitter.
  Hope you enjoyed this tutorial! If you are curious about what else you can do with Palabra or would just like to try it go ahead an  create an account here.

title_en: Using emails for retention – How to automate

美国国家航空航天局正在积极监测地球磁场中的一种奇怪的异常现象：在南美洲和西南部非洲之间延伸的地球上空磁场强度较低的巨大区域。
这种巨大的、发展中的现象被称为南大西洋异常，多年来一直吸引着科学家的兴趣和关注，也许没有人比NASA的研究人员更感兴趣了。航天局的卫星和宇宙飞船特别容易受到异常中磁场强度减弱的影响，并因此暴露在来自太阳的带电粒子中。
美国国家航空航天局(NASA)将南大西洋异常(SAA)比作地球磁场中的凹坑，或太空中的某种坑洞，通常不会影响地球上的生命，但轨道航天器(包括国际空间站)就不能说同样的话了，因为它们在低地球轨道高度绕地球旋转时，直接穿过异常区域。
在这些遭遇期间，异常内部磁场强度的减弱意味着，如果卫星上的技术系统被太阳发出的高能质子击中，它们可能会短路并发生故障。
这些随机命中通常只会产生低水平的故障，但它们确实存在导致重大数据丢失甚至关键部件永久损坏的风险-威胁迫使卫星运营商在航天器进入异常区之前例行关闭航天器系统。
减少太空中的这些危险是NASA追踪SAA的原因之一；另一个原因是，异常的奥秘代表着一个调查复杂而难以理解的现象的绝佳机会，而NASA的广泛资源和研究团队是唯一被任命来研究这一事件的。
马里兰州格林贝尔特美国宇航局戈达德太空飞行中心的地球物理学家特里·萨巴卡解释说，磁场实际上是来自许多电流源的磁场叠加而成。
主要的来源被认为是地球外核内数千公里深的熔融铁水的漩涡海洋。该物质的运动产生电流，产生地球磁场，但似乎不一定是均匀的。
位于非洲大陆下方约2900公里(1800英里)的一个巨大的致密岩石水库，叫做非洲大低切变速度省，扰乱了磁场的产生，导致了戏剧性的减弱效应-这得益于行星磁轴的倾斜。
NASA戈达德地球物理学家和数学家邝伟佳说，观测到的SAA也可以解释为该地区偶极场主导地位减弱的结果。
更具体地说，具有反转极性的局域场在SAA区强烈生长，从而使场强非常弱，比周围区域的场强弱。
虽然许多科学家仍然不能完全理解这种反常现象及其影响，但新的见解不断地揭示这一奇怪的现象。
例如，美国国家航空航天局(NASA)太阳物理学家阿什利·格里利(Ashley Greeley)在2016年领导的一项研究显示，SAA正缓慢向西北方向漂移。
然而，它不仅仅是在移动。更值得注意的是，这一现象似乎正在一分为二的过程中，研究人员今年发现，SAA似乎分裂成两个不同的细胞，每个细胞代表较大异常内的一个单独的最低磁场强度中心。
这对SAA的未来意味着什么仍不得而知，但无论如何，有证据表明这种异常现象并不是新出现的。
上个月发表的一项研究表明，这一现象不是最近发生的反常事件，而是一种反复发生的磁性事件，可能早在1100万年前就影响了地球。
如果是这样的话，这可能表明南大西洋异常并不是整个星球磁场反转的触发器或前兆，如果不是在数十万年的时间里，这种情况实际上是会发生的。
显然，巨大的问题仍然存在，但随着这一巨大的磁性怪事发生了如此多的事情，了解世界上最强大的航天局正像他们一样密切关注着它，这是一件很好的事情。
萨巴卡说，尽管SAA移动缓慢，但它在形态上正在经历一些变化，所以我们通过继续执行任务来继续观察它也是很重要的。
note_en:
NASA is actively monitoring a strange anomaly in Earth's magnetic field: a  giant region of lower magnetic intensity in the skies above the planet, stretching out between South America and southwest Africa.
   This vast, developing phenomenon, called the  South Atlantic Anomaly, has intrigued and concerned scientists for years, and perhaps none more so than NASA researchers. The space agency's satellites and spacecraft are particularly vulnerable to the weakened magnetic field strength within the anomaly, and the resulting exposure to charged particles from the Sun.
 The South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA) –  likened by NASA to a 'dent' in Earth's magnetic field, or a kind of 'pothole in space' – generally doesn't affect life on Earth, but the same can't be said for orbital spacecraft (including the International Space Station), which pass directly through the anomaly as they loop around the planet at low-Earth orbit altitudes.
 During these encounters, the reduced magnetic field strength inside the anomaly means technological systems onboard satellites can short-circuit and malfunction if they become struck by high-energy protons emanating from the Sun.
  These random hits may usually only produce low-level glitches, but they do carry the risk of causing significant data loss, or even permanent damage to key components – threats obliging satellite operators to routinely shut down spacecraft systems before spacecraft enter the anomaly zone.
 Mitigating those hazards in space is one reason NASA is tracking the SAA; another is that the mystery of the anomaly represents a great opportunity to investigate a complex and difficult-to-understand phenomenon, and NASA's broad resources and research groups are uniquely well-appointed to study the occurrence.
   "The magnetic field is actually a superposition of fields from many current sources,"  explains geophysicist Terry Sabaka from NASA's Goddard Space Flight Centre in Greenbelt, Maryland.
 The primary source is considered to be a  swirling ocean of molten iron inside Earth's outer core, thousands of kilometres below the ground. The movement of that mass generates electrical currents that create Earth's magnetic field, but not necessarily uniformly, it seems.
 A huge reservoir of dense rock called the  African Large Low Shear Velocity Province, located about 2,900 kilometres (1,800 miles) below the African continent, disturbs the field's generation, resulting in the dramatic weakening effect – which is aided by the tilt of the planet's magnetic axis.
 "The observed SAA can be also interpreted as a consequence of weakening dominance of the dipole field in the region,"  says NASA Goddard geophysicist and mathematician Weijia Kuang.
 "More specifically, a localised field with reversed polarity grows strongly in the SAA region, thus making the field intensity very weak, weaker than that of the surrounding regions."
  While there's much scientists still don't fully understand about the anomaly and its implications, new insights are continually shedding light on this strange phenomenon.
 For example,  one study led by NASA heliophysicist Ashley Greeley in 2016 revealed the SAA is drifting slowly in a north-westerly direction.
   It's not just moving, however. Even more remarkably, the phenomenon seems to be in the process of splitting in two, with researchers this year discovering that the SAA  appears to be dividing into two distinct cells, each representing a separate centre of minimum magnetic intensity within the greater anomaly.
 Just what that means for the future of the SAA remains unknown, but in any case, there's evidence to suggest that the anomaly is not a new appearance.
 A study published last month suggested the phenomenon is not a freak event of recent times, but a recurrent magnetic event that may have affected Earth  since as far back as 11 million years ago.
 If so, that could signal that the South Atlantic Anomaly is not a trigger or precursor to the  entire planet's magnetic field flipping, which is something that actually happens, if not for hundreds of thousands of years at a time.
 Obviously, huge questions remain, but with so much going on with this vast magnetic oddity, it's good to know the world's most powerful space agency is watching it as closely as they are.
 "Even though the SAA is slow-moving, it is going through some change in morphology, so it's also important that we keep observing it by having continued missions,"  says Sabaka.
title: 美国国家航空航天局正在追踪地球磁场中一个巨大的、不断增长的异常
title_en: NASA Is Tracking a Vast, Growing Anomaly in Earth's Magnetic Field

微软无疑是在与Surface二人组一起随着自己的鼓点前进。在一个千篇一律的智能手机和尖端可折叠显示设备的世界里，微软正在将两个过渡性的智能手机屏幕并排放在一起，并推动双应用程序的使用，以此作为其有史以来第一款自有品牌Android设备的使用方式。
在最初的新闻洗牌中，有一件事被遗漏了，那就是Surface Duo实际上是有史以来最薄的智能手机之一。2000年中，对纤薄的迷恋误入歧途，导致了一些被贴上世界上最薄智能手机标签的花哨设备。最高点(低点？)。纤薄之战的重头戏是Vivo X5Max，它的厚度达到了惊人的4.75毫米(你能相信它还有耳机插孔吗？)。上周，微软公布了Surface Duo的规格，该公司只是随意地将该设备打开时的厚度列为4.8 mm。当你插入Surface Duo充电时，USB-C插头会比设备的主体厚。
CNET最近通过一个不起作用的透明原型看到了Surface Duo的内部结构，照片和视频显示了微软在狂热地致力于瘦身方面走了多远。Surface Duo的主板看起来像是单面的。所有的芯片都在电路板的一侧，大概光滑的背面看起来被紧紧地压在了设备的背面。
单板结构意味着Surface主板的这个表面积绝对是巨大的。这款设备的右半部分几乎全是主板，在图像编辑器中将照片切碎，可以看到实际上有更多的总面积专门放在组件上，而不是放在电池上。主板真的很大，特别是当你考虑到两个部件中唯一额外的部件是额外的屏幕和连接两半的电线时。你可能会争辩说，这两款手机的组件实际上比大多数智能手机都少，因为整个设备只有一个摄像头。
双面电路板在大多数设备中都很常见，许多智能手机制造商已经开始将电路板堆叠在一起，让他们有三到四个平面来放置芯片。尽可能减少主板表面积会为电池留出更多空间，如果你看一部现代智能手机的拆卸，你会看到只有最小的一小块区域留给主板，现在是一个密密麻麻的小芯片三明治。
厚度将是可折叠智能手机的一个关键因素，因为将设备对折意味着将正常厚度翻一番。Galaxy Folder1完美地解释了这个问题。打开后，它是一部相当普通的7.6毫米厚的智能手机，但把它对折起来，在铰链机构上多加一点，你就会得到一块17.1毫米的砖块，你肯定会注意到它在你的口袋里。
微软表示，Surface二人组不是次要设备，它希望人们重新考虑如何使用口袋里的这款设备。薄是口袋容量的一个关键因素，但微软在这个过程中似乎有一点狭隘的眼光。折叠时，Surface Duo可能会非常薄，但也会非常宽。这款93毫米的手机比有史以来最宽的安卓手机Nexus 6宽了10毫米。考虑到摩托罗拉如此迅速地从这种尺寸的设备上撤退，大多数人认为Nexus 6太宽了。它限制了你的行动能力，因为它必须放进你的口袋-如果它甚至可以放在那里的话。我不确定建立一个色拉盘的轮廓的设备是最好的口袋方式。
目前还没有人在Surface二人组上花费任何重要的时间，但这款手机将于9月10日发货。
note_en:
Microsoft is certainly marching to the beat of its own drum with the Surface Duo. In a world of cookie-cutter slab smartphones and cutting edge foldable display devices, Microsoft is slapping two transitional smartphone screens next to each other and pushing dual-app usage as the way to use its first-ever self-branded Android device.
 Something lost in the initial news shuffle is that the Surface Duo is actually one of the thinnest smartphones ever made. The mid-2000's misguided obsession with thinness resulted in a few gimmicky devices labeled the "world's thinnest smartphone." The high point (low point?) of the thinness war was the  Vivo X5Max, which had an astounding 4.75mm thickness (and would you believe it still had a headphone jack?). Last week, Microsoft published the Surface Duo specs, and the company just casually listed "4.8mm" as the thickness of the device when open. When you plug the Surface Duo in to charge it, the USB-C plug will be thicker than the body of the device.
 CNET recently got a look at the internals of the Surface Duo via a non-functional, transparent prototype, and the  photos and  video shows just how far Microsoft went in its fanatical dedication to thinness. The Surface Duo has what looks to be a single-sided motherboard. All the chips are on one side of the board, and the presumably smooth back looks to be pressed up hard against the back of the device.
 The one-sided board construction means the, uh,  surface area of the Surface motherboard is absolutely massive. The right half of the device is nearly all motherboard, and chopping the photo up in an image editor shows there's actually more total area dedicated to components than to the battery. It really is amazing how big the motherboard is, especially when you consider the only extra components in the Duo are the extra screen and wires connecting the two halves. You could argue the Duo actually has a fewer components than most smartphones, since there's only one camera in the entire device.
 Double-sided circuit boards are common in most devices, and many smartphone manufacturers have started stacking circuit boards on top of each other, giving them three or four planes to place chips on. Minimizing the motherboard surface area as much as possible leaves more space for battery, and if you look at a teardown of a modern smartphone, you'll see only the tiniest scraps of area reserved for the motherboard, which is now a dense little chip sandwich.
  Thinness is going to be a key component of foldable smartphones, since folding a device in half means doubling the normal thickness. The Galaxy Fold 1 perfectly explains this problem. Unfolded, it's a pretty normal 7.6mm thick smartphone, but fold it in half and add a bit more for the hinge mechanism and you get a 17.1mm brick that you're definitely going to notice in your pocket.
 Microsoft says the Surface Duo is  not a secondary device, and it wants people to "rethink how they want to use the device in their pocket." Thinness is a key part of pocketability, but it seems like Microsoft got a bit of tunnel vision in the process. When folded, the Surface Duo might be extremely thin, but it is also extremely wide. At 93mm, the phone is 10mm wider than one of the widest Android phones ever, the Nexus 6. The Nexus 6 was deemed too wide by most people, given how quickly Motorola retreated from devices of this size. It limited your mobility due to having to fit in your pocket—if it even could fit in there. I am not sure building a device with the profile of a salad plate is the best approach to pocketability.
 No one has spent any significant time with the Surface Duo yet, but the  phone ships September 10.
title: Surface Duo内部设计展示了微软对纤薄的狂热承诺
title_en: Surface Duo internals show Microsoft’s fanatical commitment to thinness

下一代虚拟现实头盔还不清楚，但VR软件生态系统刚刚巩固了对大量用户的一项并不令人意外的-可能不受欢迎的-需求。
从今年10月开始，任何全新进入Oculus VR生态系统的人都将被迫使用Facebook账户登录设备，而不是创建和登录一个单独的Oculus专用账户。如果你已经有了Oculus账户(这是免费的，不需要Oculus品牌的耳机)，你可以继续为现有和新款耳机使用这些凭证，直到2023年1月1日，届时所有连接的Oculus服务(和全新的耳机)都将在没有Facebook账户的情况下无法运行。
虽然Oculus的官方声明一开始就大肆宣扬这一改变将如何改善Oculus VR体验，但最后却出现了许多Facebook批评者担心的那种信息共享和跟踪器泛滥的情况：
当您使用Facebook帐户登录Oculus时，Facebook将使用与您使用VR和其他Facebook产品相关的信息来提供和改善您的体验。此信息还用于向您显示个性化内容，包括广告。例如，我们可能会向您展示您可能喜欢的Oculus活动的推荐，关于Facebook应用程序和技术的广告，或者开发者为他们的VR应用程序做的广告。
自从Kickstarter支持的大规模发布以来，Oculus一直维护着自己的登录系统，这样用户就可以管理数字许可证、朋友列表和其他特定于VR的内容。但在2014年收购了Oculus的Facebook最终要求虚拟现实用户将他们的Facebook凭证与他们的账户联系起来。虽然这一过程通常被描述为完全可选的，但Oculus已经开始将某些内容-特别是社交内容-分享游戏视频等功能与联网的FB凭据进行关联。
对于没有Facebook账户的人来说，2023年的转换几乎没有什么好消息。到那时，从Oculus商店的角度来看，你的Oculus耳机实际上将成为一款离线设备，对于任何购买了耳机和联网软件许可证的人来说，这是一个令人担忧的消息，因为他们假设这些设备在未来几年可能都不会有Facebook。好消息是，此举可能不会影响通过SteamVR或Windows Mixed Reality等店面进行投资的Oculus用户，因为这些平台对Oculus耳机开放(尽管访问Oculus Store的非Oculus耳机就不是这样了，因为这些仍然需要粉丝做的变通办法，比如Revive)。
如果你已经享受过Oculus Quest的功能，该功能可以将流行的便携式耳机与高端电脑连接起来，这也会受到Facebook要求的影响，因为Oculus Link依赖于耳机的联网软件套件。
Facebook坚称，这一改变对于社交功能至关重要，但它的列表似乎没有提到任何特别有利于游戏开发或可用性的东西。声明还非常明确地表明，Facebook将根据你对该设备的使用来获取数据，并将这些信息专门与你的Facebook账户捆绑在一起，没有任何迹象表明，用户将能够告诉Facebook停止跟踪你的VR活动，比如你使用哪些应用程序，或者你在Facebook其他产品上的活动信息，比如你喜欢的页面和你加入的群组。
Oculus很可能会在Facebook发布Oculus硬件和软件生态系统的重大消息之前发布这一消息，因为有传言和设备图像暗示，广受欢迎的Oculus Quest耳机将在9月份的某个时候更新。我们预计Facebook在虚拟现实社交空间方面的实验届时也会有新的进展，我们上一次看到这种情况是在2019年，形式是Facebook Horizon。
note_en:
The next generation of virtual reality headsets remains unclear, but the VR software ecosystem just firmed up an unsurprising—and potentially unwelcome—requirement for a huge swath of users.
 Starting "this October," anyone brand-new to the Oculus VR ecosystem will be forced to log in to devices using a Facebook account, instead of creating and logging into a separate Oculus-specific account. If you already have an Oculus account (which is free and doesn't require an Oculus-branded headset), you can continue using those credentials for existing and new headsets until January 1, 2023, at which point all connected Oculus services (and brand-new headsets) will not function without a Facebook account.
  Though  Oculus's official announcement begins by trumpeting how this change will improve the Oculus VR experience, it concludes with the exact kind of information-sharing and tracker-heavy deluge that many  Facebook  critics  fear:
 When you log into Oculus using your Facebook account, Facebook will use information related to your use of VR and other Facebook products to provide and improve your experience. This information is also used to show you personalized content, including ads. For example, we might show you recommendations for Oculus Events you might like, ads about Facebook apps and technologies, or ads from developers for their VR apps.
 Since its  massive, Kickstarter-backed launch, Oculus has maintained its own login system so that users can manage digital licenses, friend lists, and other VR-specific content. But Facebook, who  acquired Oculus in 2014, eventually asked  VR users to connect their Facebook credentials to their accounts. While that process has been routinely described as wholly optional, Oculus has begun gating certain content—particularly "social" sharing features like gameplay videos—to connected FB credentials.
 The 2023 changeover includes very little good news for anyone who does not maintain a Facebook account. At that time, your Oculus-made headset will essentially become an offline device in terms of the Oculus Store, which is troubling news for anyone who purchased the headset and connected software licenses under the assumption that the devices may remain Facebook-free for years to come. In good news, this move will likely not affect Oculus users who invest in purchases made via storefronts like SteamVR or Windows Mixed Reality, as those platforms are open to Oculus headsets (though the same cannot be said for non-Oculus headsets accessing the Oculus Store, as these still require fan-made workarounds like Revive).
  If you've enjoyed Oculus Quest's  "Link" functionality, which connects  that popular, portable headset to higher-end PCs, this too will be affected by the Facebook requirement, since Oculus Link relies on the headset's Internet-connected software suite.
 Facebook insists that this change is essential for "social features," but its list doesn't appear to mention anything that would specifically benefit game development or usability. The announcement also makes abundantly clear that Facebook will access data based on your use of the device and tie that information specifically with your Facebook account, with zero indication that users will be able to tell Facebook to stop tracking things like "your VR activity, like which apps you use," or "information about your activity on other Facebook products, such as Pages you like and groups you join."
 Oculus may very well be getting this news out ahead of major Facebook-related announcements for the Oculus hardware and software ecosystem, as rumors and device images hint to  a refresh of the popular Oculus Quest headset coming sometime in September. We expect Facebook's experiments with VR social spaces to get an update at that time as well, which we last saw in 2019 in the form of  Facebook Horizon.
title: Oculus的Facebook化从10月份开始变得更加明显
title_en: The Facebookening of Oculus becomes more pronounced starting in October

note_en:
Today's Dealmaster is headlined by another round of  bargains on Amazon devices, with several Fire TV media streamers, Fire tablets, Kindle ebook readers, and Echo smart speakers all discounted as part of a sale aimed at back-to-school shoppers. While most of the offers do not match the all-time lows we typically see on Black Friday and Amazon Prime Day, many of them match the best prices we've seen outside of those special shopping events.
 The highlights include the  Fire TV Stick 4K for $35, which matches the best price we've seen on the 4K- and HDR-ready media streamer this year. Though it's not as technically capable as a higher-end box like the Apple TV 4K or Nvidia Shield TV Pro,  we still think Amazon's highest-end streaming stick is a good value for those who need more apps than a smart TV or game console can provide on their own. It's not perfect: its interface still has redundancy issues and gives favor to Amazon's own services, while  negotiating disputes have kept official versions of apps like Peacock and HBO Max off the Fire TV platform entirely. But its hardware is still fast, its voice search is effective, it supports all the major HDR standards, and, if nothing else, its UI isn't as visually bland as something like Roku's OS. Plus, with a  little extra effort, it's possible to sideload those missing apps on a Fire TV device, which isn't possible with Roku streamers.
 Elsewhere, all the major Fire tablet deals  we highlighted last week are still live, while the Kindle Paperwhite,  our favorite ebook reader,  is $30 off. If you're OK with having a smart speaker in your home, almost all of the major Echo devices are on sale as well. Again, Black Friday is three months away, and Amazon  has confirmed that Prime Day is happening sometime in the fourth quarter, so if you can hold off a few more months, we'll likely see lower prices on most of these devices by the end of the year. note:
伊萨克·罗斯(Issac Roth)是一位经验丰富的企业家，他为创始人提供开源技术方面的建议，并保持社区的参与度。在他的职业生涯中，他创建并出售了多家企业软件公司，并作为顾问和投资者保持活跃。
我自己也被解雇过几次，一次是在Pivoting初创公司，另一次是在2001年的经济低迷时期，所以我知道那是什么感觉。我也曾在一家裁员的大公司当过经理，所以我看过公司的策略。但作为首席执行官，我亲自向这些人推销我们的愿景，几个月甚至几年都和他们一起挤在一个不合格的小办公室里-这感觉非常个人化。那时候，就业市场很强劲：我不担心团队成员找到新工作。今天的不确定性更大。
由于许多初创企业面临大流行引发的经济危机的压力，我采访了几位不得不策划与新冠肺炎相关的裁员的首席执行官，以捕捉(我认为)正确和富有同情心地缩减规模的一些最佳实践。
一家公司的产品发布待定，但由于新冠肺炎的不确定性，一些续签被推迟。与此同时，董事会决定延长跑道，以便有更多的选择。问题是：公司是否应该完成产品发布，并让员工知道他们在产品发布后失去了工作？或者他们应该提前告诉员工，冒着失去重点的风险，而团队中的一些成员(正确地)开始找工作？
note_en:
Issac Roth is a seasoned entrepreneur who advises founders on open source technology and keeping communities engaged. Over this career, he’s created and sold multiple enterprise software companies and stays active as an advisor and investor.
  I’d been laid off a few times myself, once from a pivoting startup and again during the downturn of 2001, so I knew what it felt like. I’d also been a manager at a larger company that laid off employees, so I’d seen the corporate playbook. But as the CEO, I had personally sold these people on our vision, cramming into a small substandard office with them for months or years — it felt very personal. Back then, the job market was robust: I didn’t worry about team members finding new jobs. Today is more uncertain.
 With many startups under the pressure of a pandemic-fueled economic crisis, I interviewed several CEOs who have had to orchestrate COVID-19-related layoffs to capture (what I believe) are some best practices to downsize correctly and compassionately.
  One company had a pending product launch, yet a few renewals were pushed due to COVID-19-based uncertainty. Meanwhile, the board had decided to extend runway to have more options. The question was: Should the company complete the product launch and let employees know they’re losing their jobs after? Or should they tell employees ahead of time, risking a loss in focus while some members of the team (correctly) start looking for jobs?
title: 缩小规模的“正确”方式
